item.title: Have a Great Wednesday Imgur!
item.description:
I miss these surrealist celebrity photo shoots from the 90s/early 2000s, they elicited the image of an unhinged/feral photographer on 27 cups of coffee and no sleep shouting for progressively weirder props while encouraging the celebrity into increasingly more suggestive or chaotic poses.
I despise looking-at-towels.
I wanted the role of Mercutio so badly due to him in highschool-he inspired me to join drama class.
Front Page Edit: Hi! I hate to ask, but our incredibly awesome pup Scotia has to get a surgery, and I was layed off due to "Economic Downward Trend" last month. Anything helps, and he gets a belly rub for every donation-thank you! :) https://gofund.me/0c1e0bfa
item.dc:creator: huntsnhava40

item.title: Like someone was trying to describe what a human woman looks like.
item.description: I feel like they don't even look human.
item.dc:creator: hazelismycatdog

item.title: I bought this huge rough opal 4 years ago and finally decided to cut it
item.description:
Let me begin by letting you know that this type of Ethiopian opal is unlike the hydrophane opal (https://www.moregems.com/collections/ethiopian-welo-opal-gemstones) from Ethiopia which is beautiful, vibrant, stable and wearable in jewelry. This on the other hand, is a type of non-Hydrophane crystal opal and is a collector gem which can not be worn in jewelry. I say collector gem because it must he stored moist. If it is allowed to dry out for an extended period of time, the surface will begin to craze (crack). At the time about 4-5 years ago, the material was fairly new and this rough was sold to us dry and it looked very promising. It was very transparent (in comparison to the Hydrophane material we were used to) and had some interesting colors to it. I cut a couple of the smaller ones first (shown below) and they were stable for a few months to almost a year, but then began to craze. It was a bummer because one of them was already sold and I had a mounting for it. When it was about to be set, our jeweler noticed the surface was starting to craze. That said, I really didn’t feel up to cutting the larger one until now. I knew it would be an interesting gem when finished, so I figured if I store it wet, it should remain in tact. Unfortunately at this time, we dont have any techinques to stablize the crystal opal material from Ethiopia. It is an amazing and beautiful faceted gem that can be appreciated for what it is!
This was the same gem we bought dry about 4 years ago, it was an impressive piece of rough that we had high hopes for. Our supplier was pretty confident that it would be stable. Unfortunately, we’re pretty sure this gem will have the same fate as the ones below if we leave it in open air for an extended period of time without water. Once we saw the others starting to craze, we wrapped this piece of rough in wet paper towel and bagged it up to keep it from drying out before we would have a chance to cut it. Four years later, it is now a faceted gem.
This was another amazing piece of the non-hydrophane crystal Opal rough from Ethiopia. It was the first that we cut. It was also sold to us dry, which at the time was an exciting find.
This was the gem we got from it. It was pretty amazing and unlike anything that we had cut before. This stone held up for 7-8 months and then unfortunately it started to craze.
This was another gem from a similar piece of rough that was sold to us dry.
This is the fate of the non-Hydrophane material. This one started to craze a month or two after cutting and this is it now, 3-4 years later with the surface entirely crazed. This is what we believe will happen to the large stone if we don’t keep it moist. Lately, if you see this material, it will typically be sold wet and it must stay wet or your opal will end up like this. That said, this material should not be confused with the Hydrophane Ethiopian Opal material (still sold dry), which is stable and is good for jewelry.
This big 198 carat gem will have to be stored in a moist container, only to be removed for brief periods to appreciate its beauty. A collector stone that is not suitable for jewelry. While we wish it was stable— we’re still very happy with the end result, are glad to have had the chance to cut it, and believe it will be a unique addition to someone’s gem collection. I hope you enjoyed seeing the process and a few of these interesting faceted non-Hydrophane crystal opals from Ethiopia. If you have any questions about this gem, be sure to tune in to our next live streaming gem show early September, where we will be releasing this gem for sale and discussing it at the show here: https://www.youtube.com/live/m7xFxKEeHpc?si=n-wkkLB0zg0ciWSQ Let us know what you think in the comments!
item.dc:creator: MoreGems

snoop's feet
give this bird a part in forrest gump 2
yes
talk about a plot hole
my mom is usually pretty cool
as is tradition
not with boring names like 'A' and 'B'
dude, oversharing
how hearty is this stew?
trout
mmm hmm
i'm old and remember this time
i guess
we've all done this
accurate
real talk
that's all for me.. have a great night, gang!!

item.title: Please don't let this die in usersub, The news isn't reporting this because the reporters don't understand it
item.description: Good article from a researcher: https://www.troyhunt.com/inside-the-3-billion-people-national-public-data-breach/ Check if you are in there: https://npd.pentester.com/search In short, It isn't 3 billion people, its 100's of millions, which has multiple rows per person. This is from a background check company, so when you apply for anything that verifies your identity, this database and ones like it are used to verify who you are. It was stolen in April 2024, offered for sale this summer and is now freely public data on the darkweb. Do you see the problem? My dad got his bank account drained and when he called the bank, the agent said that all the info given to steal his money were given correctly by the thief, so they aren't going to reimburse him. How do you protect yourself? If you are protected by GDPR, you don't appear to be here. If you have a delete me from the internet service, you are likely not in here. Everyone should call their banks/ credit companies and make sure that they don't allow people to use your stuff out of state at the least. And Freeze your credit so no one can open accounts in your name without knowing: https://www.investopedia.com/how-to-freeze-and-unfreeze-your-credit-5075527 Use a service like deleteme to clean up your info on the web if possible. Get LifeLock to monitor your information. Freezing credit and talking to your banks/card companies and checking your purchase history are mandatory.
item.dc:creator: downrightmike

item.title: Didn't she say she was willing to go to jail to defend her views? Somehow it doesn't look that way!
item.dc:creator: blank666man

item.title: Little job secret that not a lot of people are aware of
item.description: So years ago, my husband and I were looking for a new job for him. Something that he would enjoy but made good money (1 in a million, right?). After he looked into the usual electrician, plumbing, or welding trade jobs, he kept striking out and it was hard on his emotional state. I was determined to help him find a career, not another dead end job to jump into. This lead to the discovery of....PEST CONTROL! HOLY CRAP. Okay, so let me give you some PROS for the pest control market. 1. 100% Job security, there will ALWAYS be pests. 2. People pay a LOT of money to not deal with bugs. 3. 90% of the job is pest prevention, 10% is actual bug/pest killing 4. He says its like hunting every day. 5. Having ADHD/OCD helps you do such a good job, you'll notice tiny details. 6. Basic math for chemical/water ratio when treating. 7. Nice paid training. 8. Usually get your own work truck & gas card. 9. All pest control licenses/classes that the company pays you to learn/take are YOURS, and go with you EVEN IF YOU LEAVE THE COMPANY as long as you keep them up to date each year (a company will pay for that too) 10. One of the few corporate jobs that honest-to-god pays you what you're worth and invests in you. 11. Max benefits usually (with corporate), and you get your weekends to yourself. 12. More jobs = more pay 13. Become the thing that wasps & spiders fear. You are their end. Some CONS of the pest control market, because its only fair. 1. Some companies focus more on residential or commercial jobs. * residential can be easier, but not as much money * commercial is a lot more money, but you'll need to be more thorough 2. Do NOT accept a pest control offer that pays you less than 40k/year. * You are LITERALLY a trained POISON SPECIALIST. * Some tiny companies will screw you, and its either because they are not charging enough for the services, or they are just straight not paying you what you're worth. 3. Residential jobs will require you to sometimes go under houses. This both hurts your back/knees or make anyone with claustrophobia panic. 4. If you do not pay attention to your chemical/water ratios, and you are working under your license (that you worked so hard for), YOU are going to be responsible if any toxicity accidents happen with the Dept of Agriculture. * So be a big boy/girl and don't cut corners, pay basic attention to what you're mixing. Some other tips for the pest control market. 1. If you are a woman, you will usually have your pick of anything, but please do us women proud. It's a sausage fest up in there. 2. READ. THE. LABEL. When learning a new chemical, take the time and just read the label fully. 3. Some of the best nationwide corporations to work for are EcoLab, Ehrlich, Orkin and Terminix. Happy Bug Hunting
item.dc:creator: dorkyducks

item.title: "It's just a phase "
item.dc:creator: ProfessorGigglesworth

item.title: Divine providence?
item.description:
So this is a follow up post to the one I made some weeks ago. https://imgur.com/gallery/U2eq3UX I thought I was 11 weeks pregnant, turns out the foetus had no heartbeat & had stopped growing week 7. The ex broke up in week 6. Coincidence? Make of that what you will. I've had pregnancy symptoms the whole time. And because I don't know if this is a blessing in disguise, or just another form of grief I'm not familiar with, please enjoy the memes my sick, dark, twisted mind could wring out while bleeding my innards out on the sofa.
So yea, I'm numb from all the twists & turns from these padt 5 weeks. At least I'm fully free of the ex (finally moving out on Sat!) and able to pursue my future as I see fit. But damn, what a price to pay. I keep trying to think of this as the kid rejected his father right back. Because that feels comforting somehow. Original post: When life gives you lemons... https://imgur.com/gallery/U2eq3UX .
item.dc:creator: Mempress

item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: Parenting Win
item.description: I was excited to get the call. I'm not naive and I've been telling them for years that it's okay to call. No questions will be asked and they'll not be judged - just don't get behind the wheel if you're drunk or stoned. Paid off tonight - this was their first party after getting their license and they texted and asked for a ride. Not often we get to see the fruits of our labor like this!!
item.dc:creator: cgschlangen

item.title: “In the 1980s,musician Daryl Davis set out to understand the Ku Klux Klan. So he started befriending members at Klan rallies and joined an all-white country band. Then, he met Imperial Wizard Roger Kelly. Davis spent years building a relationship
item.description: “In the 1980s, musician Daryl Davis set out to understand the Ku Klux Klan. So he started befriending members at Klan rallies and joined an all-white country band. Then, he met Imperial Wizard Roger Kelly. Davis spent years building a relationship with Kelly, establishing trust and, eventually, a friendship. They ate meals at each others' homes while Davis listened to Kelly, seeking to see how he could hate a person like Davis without even knowing him. Eventually, Kelly started listening to Davis and left the Klan altogether, giving Davis his robes and hood. And over the past 30 years, Davis has now convinced over 200 former Klan members to walk away.”
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: Mother reunites with her son after she got the call he regained consciousness after spending 16 days in a coma
item.description: 8-year-old Guilherme was diagnosed at birth with a rare skin condition called dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa. The condition is characterized by a lack of type VII collagen, a protein that joins the dermis to the epidermis, known as ‘binding protein’. News of the young one waking up reached the mother’s ears, prompting an immediate and heartfelt rush to her child’s side and their happiness translated to everyone in that room.
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: "Shot Down in a Free Country"
item.description: Jerry Clower photo: Public Domain Jerry Clower in 1974 By Top Billing INC - ebay.com, back of photo, front of photo, Public Domain, https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=30165483 Sauce: Jerry Clower, Ain’t God Good! (Waco, TX: Word Books, Publisher, 1975), p. 72. “Howard Gerald ‘Jerry’ Clower (September 28, 1926 – August 24, 1998) was an American stand-up comedian. Born and raised in the state of Mississippi, Clower was best known for his stories of the rural South and was given the nickname ‘The Mouth of Mississippi’.” https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jerry_Clower

item.title: Oh no
item.description: Thank you everyone who supported user @akalse on his post: https://imgur.com/gallery/787rwuO/comment/2411824305 This imgurian has been going through a lot with fighting vet insurance companies and ended up winning some back. I tried to help as much as I could and spread awareness, we did get some help so Ill quit posting about it for now. Thank you everyone who supported this cause. Go hug your pets!
item.dc:creator: Rikyii
